old_claim.note: Basic Woodworking is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No woodworking edge remains because the scoped Tell es-Sultan evidence is a stone wall, ditch, tower, and staircase; the inspected sources identify no timber component or woodworking lineage.

source_shape.source_locator: Brief description identifies the site's relevant monumental features as a wall with ditch and tower, while the nomination describes the preserved Neolithic system as stone construction.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The scoped PPNA enclosure evidence is stone architecture with no documented wooden defensive component.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Woodworking existed earlier but chronological priority alone cannot create a lineage when the target construction does not use or evidence wood.

ontology_before: All early fortification forms inherited woodworking because generic palisades were listed in the broad description.
ontology_after: The Tell es-Sultan record follows its stone architecture without an unevidenced timber prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: early_fortifications no longer directly depends on woodworking_basic.
would_reject_if: Excavated evidence established a constitutive wooden structure in the PPNA wall-and-tower installation.
short_reason: The scoped installation is stone and does not evidence woodworking.
